perturbation.original_text: 2. Term of Employment 2.1 The Executive's employment with the Company will commence on the Commencement Date and shall continue, subject to the remaining terms of this Agreement, until terminated by either party giving the other the following minimum advance written notice: 2.1.1 6 weeks' notice; 2.1.2 such other longer period as required by law.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Term of Employment 2.1 The Executive's employment with the Company will commence on the Commencement Date and shall continue, subject to the remaining terms of this Agreement, until terminated by either party giving the other the following minimum advance written notice: 2.1.1 6 weeks' notice; 2.1.2 such other longer period as required by law. However, the company reserves the right to terminate the contract with no notice period under special circumstances.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating in Section 2.1 that termination requires a minimum 6 weeks' notice, but then adding a caveat that the company can terminate with no notice period under "special circumstances." This directly conflicts with the previously stated requirement for minimum notice and makes the enforcement of notice period uncertain. The conflict is particularly evident when read in conjunction with section 17, which specifies how termination and payment in lieu of notice will occur.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 17. Payment in Lieu of Notice 17.1 The Company may, in its sole and absolute discretion, terminate the Executive's employment under this Agreement at any time and with immediate effect by notifying the Executive that the Company is exercising its right under this clause 17 and that it will make a payment in l ieu of not ice ("PILON") to the Executive. The Executive's employment will terminate immediately and any PILON due to the Executive in accordance with the provisions of this clause 17 will be paid within 28 days. The PILON will be equal to the basic salary (as at the Termination Date) which the Executive would have been entitled to receive under this Agreement during the notice period referred to at clause 2 (or, if notice has already been given, during the remainder of the notice period) less deductions required by law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction because Section 2.1 initially states that termination requires a minimum 6 weeks' notice (or longer if required by law). The addition of 'However, the company reserves the right to terminate the contract with no notice period under special circumstances' creates a direct conflict. This contradicts Section 17, which outlines 'Payment in Lieu of Notice' based on the notice period referred to in clause 2. The added clause creates uncertainty as to when the 6 weeks' notice (and corresponding PILON) is applicable. Since Section 2.1 states a minimum notice and Section 17 defines PILON based on this section, the addition of a no-notice termination clause directly contradicts the defined calculation for PILON.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Salary 7.1 The Executive shall be paid a basic salary of £448,903 per annum subject to deductions required by law. The Executive's salary shall accrue from day to day, shall be payable in equal monthly instalments in arrears on or about the last day of each month. 7.2 The salary paid to the Executive will be reviewed on or around 1 April 2021. The Company is under no obligation to award an increase following a salary review.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Salary 7.1 The Executive shall be paid a basic salary of £448,903 per annum subject to deductions required by law. The Executive's salary shall accrue from day to day, shall be payable in equal monthly instalments in arrears on or about the last day of each month. Notwithstanding, in the event the Executive fails to meet certain KPIs, the salary will be reduced to £300,000. 7.2 The salary paid to the Executive will be reviewed on or around 1 April 2021. The Company is under no obligation to award an increase following a salary review.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text in Section 7 introduces an in-text contradiction. It initially states a fixed annual salary of £448,903, but then adds a clause that the salary will be reduced to £300,000 if certain KPIs are not met. This creates uncertainty and is contradicting because the initial agreement states a salary, while the other one reduces the amount if KPIs are not met. This affects section 17, which is being contradicted, because in this section they discuss the salary in case of payment in l ieu of not ice ("PILON").
perturbation.contradicted_text: 17. Payment in Lieu of Notice 17.1 The Company may, in its sole and absolute discretion, terminate the Executive's employment under this Agreement at any time and with immediate effect by notifying the Executive that the Company is exercising its right under this clause 17 and that it will make a payment in l ieu of not ice ("PILON") to the Executive. The Executive's employment will terminate immediately and any PILON due to the Executive in accordance with the provisions of this clause 17 will be paid within 28 days. The PILON will be equal to the basic salary (as at the Termination Date) which the Executive would have been entitled to receive under this Agreement during the notice period referred to at clause 2 (or, if notice has already been given, during the remainder of the notice period) less deductions required by law.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a condition where the Executive's salary can be reduced based on KPI performance. This directly contradicts Section 17, which stipulates that the PILON will be equal to the basic salary at the termination date. The modification introduces a variable salary depending on KPI achievement, which conflicts with the fixed salary assumption in the PILON clause. Therefore, there's a strong in-text contradiction that would cause confusion on how to calculate the basic salary during termination.

perturbation.original_text: 12. Holidays 12.1 The Executive shall be entitled to 28 days' paid holiday in each holiday year (which includes public holidays) to be taken at times convenient to the Company and authorised in advance. 12.2 The Company's holiday year runs from 1 January to 31 December. If the Executive's employment commences or terminates part way through a holiday year, the Executive's entitlement during that holiday year shall be calculated on a pro rata basis rounded up to the nearest whole day. 12.3 The Executive shall have no entitlement to any payment in lieu of accrued but untaken holiday except on Termination. The amount of such payment in lieu shall be 1/260 of the Executive's salary for each untaken day of entitlement.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. Holidays 12.1 The Executive shall be entitled to 28 days' paid holiday in each holiday year (which includes public holidays) to be taken at times convenient to the Company and authorised in advance. However, the company may request the Executive to work during their holiday, and this will be considered as part of their job description. 12.2 The Company's holiday year runs from 1 January to 31 December. If the Executive's employment commences or terminates part way through a holiday year, the Executive's entitlement during that holiday year shall be calculated on a pro rata basis rounded up to the nearest whole day. 12.3 The Executive shall have no entitlement to any payment in lieu of accrued but untaken holiday except on Termination. The amount of such payment in lieu shall be 1/260 of the Executive's salary for each untaken day of entitlement.
perturbation.explanation: The addition to section 12.1 creates an in-text contradiction. Initially, the section grants the Executive 28 days of paid holiday. However, the added sentence, "However, the company may request the Executive to work during their holiday, and this will be considered as part of their job description," contradicts the guaranteed holiday entitlement. By requiring work during holidays as part of the job, the company undermines the Executive's right to take those days off, as established. This directly impacts section 19, which describes the Garden Leave, because there is no clarification if Holiday days will count during Garden Leave.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 19.2 During any period of Garden Leave: ... 19.2.6 the Executive shall be contactable during each working day (except during any periods taken as holiday in the usual way);
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. Section 12.1 initially grants the Executive 28 days of paid holiday. The added sentence, "However, the company may request the Executive to work during their holiday, and this will be considered as part of their job description," contradicts the guaranteed holiday entitlement. This undermines the Executive's right to take those days off. This directly conflicts with section 19.2.6 stating "the Executive shall be contactable during each working day (except during any periods taken as holiday in the usual way)", because the change now allows work during previously protected holiday time. The addition to 12.1 now creates a situation where the executive may be required to work on a day previously considered a holiday, causing an in-text contradiction with 19.2.6.
